evidence.text: Competitive compensation and good benefits Good company to work for overall
Company is a big promoter of work life balance. Lots of paid holidays and vacation is great. Great place to start your career. Work life balance good, pay, not so much
Flexibility, mentorship, Good work-life balance Good work-life balance
I like work life balance here and the flexibility I have in my job. Decent benefits and work life balance
Great work life balance and nice people Great work life balance

Amazing benefits : 401k matching up to 4%, medical, dentist and eye health insurance, unlimited Pto, employee wellness days during summer, hybrid work place - 2 days a week at the office. Recognition points $$. Christmas party, just to name a few. Great company with great benefits.
good benefits good benefits good benefits good benefits

The company is excellent to work for. The culture is extraordinary and relaxed. They have several events to socialize and great opportunities to learn through e-learning platforms. They have significant benefits, such as a 401k match. Great culture and benefits
Ok benefits as per industry standards. Bonus is average Benefits
Great benefits: unlimited PTO, good health insurance, amazing parental leave Great company
Opportunity of work on exciting new technology in Super Compute / AI field. Flexible / (relatively) relaxed work environment Opportunity to work on exciting tech, Flexible Env but limited pay

evidence.role: Current employee - Product Marketing Manager/Senior PMM
evidence.text: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has been an influential player in the technology industry for many years. Here are some positive aspects commonly associated with working at HPE: Innovation Legacy: HPE has a long history of technological innovation, which can be inspiring for employees who are passionate about technology. Global Reach: Being a global company, HPE offers opportunities to work on international project...
Some of my coworkers at this company are impressive both technically and interpersonally. This is the highlight. Many Obstacles, Poor Leadership, Disposable Employees
HPE work culture is nice. They generally don't grind you down like other places. Nice place, but no career growth
Great tech stack to work with. Good long term growth Great tech
Collaborative environment with very intelligent employees. Friendly and Sharp people
A fantastic group of colleagues and professionals. A great group of colleagues

Working in sales at HPE is awesome because you get to sell innovative tech, build great customer relationships, grow your career, and work with a really supportive team! Streamline Support for Sales Success!
Structured, Fair and Supportive at all levels Stable and Supportive
Colleagues could be supportive when not being pitted against each other Old school business and mgmt

Sometimes upper level management get things right. Projects are sometimes interesting. Pay and benefits are very good by not crazy like the FAANGs but still very fair. When I worked for HPE the first time it was boring in a good way. No surprises, no weird power trips by managers but I moved to the Cray product line and .... see negatives. Beware legacy work areas
Some talented individual contributors, but thatâs about it. Disappointing Experience at HPE - Would Not Recommend!
Integrity is central to the culture and company wants to reward loyalty Big Corporate US Company
Dynamic and supportive environment, great management. Great company
Stable job and good environment Good Company
Low pay pretty slow growth Good company with stability but low pay
Out of pocket costs but still competitive with the market Benefits

Non technical leaders proposing half baked strategies on countering AWS and Azure that's eating their lunch Too process heavy managed by technically clueless managers lacking fire, aptitude and urgency of delivering Poor profit margins leading to low cash and financial management not competitive with top tier tech companies. low pay for junior levels not attracting talents, too top heavy orgs Very poor IT services a...

HPE does not provide as many benefits as Hewlett Packard used to back in the day. The employee stock purchase program pales in comparison and other benefits have been reduced or cut altogether. However, they still offer a reasonable array of benefits, they just cost the employee more than they used to. Great career opportunities at HPE
Terrible management. Heavy favoritism. Underpaid and overworked. Okay place to work
You don't like if you want to work in fast paced environment. Pretty good work life balance

Didn't have the concept to work life balance or 40 hours work week, you work endlessly, very un organize, didn't follow any methodology or scrum or agile or water fall.... Not a company for work life balance
Salary is not very competitive Amazing work life balance
Didn't see a lot of opportunities for growth Great Work/Life Balance
Hard to run a family in this infaltion No money but work life balance is there
average compensation, not much growth or interesting projects lately Great WLB, but not much growth in career
Not the most cutting-edge/high tech place. Developers are clearly an undervalued asset. Not a lot of room for growth Good Start, Long-term Drag
Nobody seems to know anything about your job, the future, or what is even currently going on. Nobody communicates Good opportunity to get into IT
Little structure within teams with no clear career path Great stepping stone
Not my desired career path. Applied for a different role and got placed into this one. MSM at HPE
Politics and layoffs and favoritism. Engineering led company. Not worth it

evidence.text: Business Units do not work together, products compete and the result is negative results for both products. Slow and steady, but Politics are a problem
